title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined

input_row.title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined
input_row.description: Use network policies to isolate traffic in your cluster network.
input_row.rationale: Running different applications on the same Kubernetes cluster creates a risk of one compromised application attacking a neighboring application. Network segmentation is important to ensure that containers can communicate only with those they are supposed to. A network policy is a specification of how selections of pods are allowed to communicate with each other and other network endpoints. Network Policies are namespace scoped. When a network policy is introduced to a given namespace, all traffic not allowed by the policy is denied. However, if there are no network policies in a namespace all traffic will be allowed into and out of the pods in that namespace.
input_row.audit: Run the below command and review the NetworkPolicy objects created in the cluster. kubectl --all-namespaces get networkpolicy Ensure that each namespace defined in the cluster has at least one Network Policy.
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation and create NetworkPolicy objects as you need them.
input_row.impact: Once network policies are in use within a given namespace, traffic not explicitly allowed by a network policy will be denied. As such it is important to ensure that, when introducing network policies, legitimate traffic is not blocked.
input_row.default_value: By default, network policies are not created.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions using `kubectl` to check for the presence of `NetworkPolicy` objects in all namespaces, which indicates that this can be verified through Kubernetes APIs and CLI commands.
- Network policies are a standard Kubernetes resource that can be queried directly.
- The audit process involves checking each namespace for at least one `NetworkPolicy`, which is a straightforward API call.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_network_policy_all_namespaces_defined

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl --all-namespaces get networkpolicy` to retrieve all `NetworkPolicy` objects across all namespaces.
- Enumerate the namespaces and check if each one has at least one `NetworkPolicy` defined.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: Each namespace has at least one `NetworkPolicy`.
  - Fail: Any namespace is found without a `NetworkPolicy`.
- Example: If the output of the command shows that the `default` namespace has no `NetworkPolicy`, it would indicate a compliance failure for that namespace.
